programmes.show_description: Eccentric dancer Marina wants a confidence-boosting tattoo cover to get her back on the podium. Identical twins Hayley and Rebecca have matching bad body art.
The five comics go undercover as they try to impress Taskmaster Greg Davies with their attempts at high-level espionage. And Ed Gamble shaves his knees.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Sean Lock and Roisin Conaty take on guest captain Alan Carr and Joe Lycett. Spencer Jones joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With maths whizz Rachel Riley.
Teleshopping
Teleshopping
Paul Damico, president of Moe's Southwest Grill, one of the world's fastest-growing restaurant chains, goes undercover to find out how efficient his organisation is.
Sara Bittorf, the chief brand officer of Boston Market - whose 470 restaurants serve home-style comfort food - goes undercover to work alongside the chain's employees.
There's celebrity name-dropping, Victorian hair clippings, bromance, fishcake-related bust-ups and wedding dance hilarity as three couples compete to host the best party in Wiltshire.
In East Anglia, Lisa and Steven rustle up a roast on board their boat, self-confessed snobs Justine and Ben serve classy Italian, and Rosa and Darren source their ingredients from Spain.
...Selling: Andrea and Santino's dream of moving back downtown is a waking nightmare until the brothers step in with massive renovations.
The One on a Caravan Site: Lauren wants to feel unique at her wedding, but her groom plans their nuptials at the same caravan park that Lauren visits every year...
The Titanic One: Essex couple Jas and Bobby are heading for rocky waters. One of them wants a quiet wedding, while the other favours a Titanic-style night to remember.
Paul Quentel, the president of Alfred Angelo, the second-largest bridal retailer in the USA, poses as a reality TV contestant to go undercover in his own company.
Mike Bloom, the president and COO of discount chain Family Dollar, goes incognito to find out where the firm is excelling and where it needs improvement.
Four Seasons Inn: Gordon Ramsay visits West Dover in Vermont, where a dog-friendly hotel is run like a hostel - with unpaid staff, instead of paying guests, staying in the rooms.
Murphy's Historic Hotel: Gordon checks in to Murphys Historic Hotel in northern California, where the owners are distracted from business by the party going on in the rowdy bar.
and Selling: Brand new series: As Jonathan tackles firefighter Ryan and Lesley's turf trouble, will Drew be too busy with a firefighter fitness challenge?
My Super Sideways Sixteen: Daisy confronts chef Cloyce about his underwhelming meals. Will he sink or swim with a sweet 16 celebration?
The grand final features tip-top teas, preposterous chickpeas and absent keys. But who will become only the ninth ever Taskmaster series champion?
...Christmas Special: Jimmy Carr hosts as Sean Lock and Kathy Burke take on Jon Richardson and David Mitchell. James Acaster and Joe Wilkinson join Susie Dent and Rachel Riley.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Sean Lock and Johnny Vegas take on guest captain Alan Carr and Lou Sanders. The Brett Domino Trio join Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.
The Cage: Jim sets up a giant dog cage for Milson in the Goodmans' living room, and Aunty Val's got a hot date with a 'sex robot'.
The Females: Adam and Jonny bring their 'females' home to meet the parents. Cue an overexcited Mum and an extremely embarrassing Dad.
Ladies' man Ash needs help covering an embarrassing portrait of his beloved mum. Sean wants a cover for his leg tattoo of two dogs doing what comes naturally.
The grand final features tip-top teas, preposterous chickpeas and absent keys. But who will become only the ninth ever Taskmaster series champion?
Jimmy Carr hosts as Sean Lock and Johnny Vegas take on guest captain Alan Carr and Lou Sanders. The Brett Domino Trio join Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.
Teleshopping
Teleshopping
CEO Andy Wirth takes on a new identity to work incognito among his own employees at a gigantic ski resort near Lake Tahoe.
Fatburger, based in Los Angeles, is known as the 'last great hamburger stand'. CEO Andy Wiederhorn dons a disguise to go undercover in his own organisation.
On the north Kent coast, the dining contest features an egg phobia, a banter battle, an impromptu proposal and a haka. And one contestant puts his foot in it with his own fiancée - twice.
In East Sussex, there's rock-solid banoffee pie and obscure meats including pig's trotter and ox cheek, as three couples compete to host the best dinner party.
...Selling: Fire chief Joe and his two daughters are stoked to move out of their bland home. But can he find a new place that sparks his girls' interest?
The One in a Mate's Field: Sophia wants a blingy wedding. Love-struck groom Micky hopes to impress with a winter wonderland event. Will his wedding plan get a frosty reception?
The Hot Tub One: While bride-to-be Hayley always dreamed of a traditional Jewish celebration, bodylicious Ben would prefer a hot tub wedding. Will Hayley take the plunge?
Travis Boersma, co-founder and president of US drive-through coffee shop chain Dutch Bros, does the daily grind undercover alongside his staff.
Ex-undercover boss Eric Casaburi brings together six participants in the series who were 'busted', 'nearly busted' or 'self-busted' while working in disguise among their staff.
Curtis House Inn: Gordon visits Connecticut's oldest inn, the Curtis House. Run by siblings who no longer talk to each other, things are falling apart. Will their legacy be lost?
Historic Calumet Inn: Gordon visits the struggling Historic Calumet Inn in Pipestone, Minnesota, where the overworked staff are on the brink of revolt.
...and Selling: Brand new series: Grab your Tamagotchi's and oversized flannel tees because Craig and Simone's home is stuck in the 90s.
An Eye for Decline: It's all hands on deck to prevent a nautical crisis in the middle of the night. And is the yacht life too much for one crew member?
Greg Davies and Alex Horne pit winners Josh Widdicombe, Katherine Ryan, Rob Beckett, Noel Fielding and Bob Mortimer against each other in the ultimate test. (Ep1/2)
Jimmy Carr hosts as Sean Lock and Claudia Winkleman take on Jon Richardson and Henning Wehn. Nick Helm joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With maths whizz Rachel Riley.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Sean Lock and Joe Wilkinson take on guest captain Richard Ayoade and Aisling Bea. Tom Allen joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.
Warring Factions: Mark sets himself the task of sleeping with Toni from next door, basing his seduction technique on the Battle of Stalingrad...
The Interview: Jeremy and Super Hans' band, the Hair Blair Bunch, is going nowhere - so Jeremy reluctantly agrees to go for a job working in Mark's office...
